subject_tmpl: Basement flooding after last night's rain — {ADDRESS}
body_tmpl: Hi, I'm {FIRST} {LAST} at {ADDRESS}. Last night's storm sent water into my basement through the floor drain — about {NUM} inches standing by morning. This is the third time since {DATE}. My neighbor at the same block says his place did the same thing. Is there a sewer capacity issue on our block? I'd really appreciate someone coming out to look at what's going on before the next heavy rain. My number is {PHONE}.
reply_tmpl: Thank you for reaching out, {FIRST}. We've logged a service request for {ADDRESS} and will have a crew inspect the sewer main on your block within {DAYS} business days. If you experience additional flooding before then, please call Public Works at 708-547-3500. We appreciate your patience.

subject_tmpl: Clogged storm drain at {STREET} and {CROSS}
body_tmpl: {FIRST} {LAST} here, resident on {STREET}. The catch basin at the corner of {STREET} and {CROSS} has been completely blocked with leaves and debris since at least {DATE}. Every time it rains, that whole intersection ponds up past the curb and water runs into the first three driveways on {STREET}. Can someone get out there to clear it? It's a hazard for pedestrians too.

subject_tmpl: Ongoing ponding problem on {STREET} — please help
body_tmpl: Mayor Okonkwo — I've lived on {STREET} for {AGE} years and the low spot near {CROSS} has always ponded, but it's getting worse every season. After the {DATE} storm the water sat for {DAYS} days. My kids can't use the sidewalk and cars are driving through it at speed and splashing porches. I've called the village twice and nothing has changed. I'm asking you personally to look into a permanent fix. — {FIRST} {LAST}, {ADDRESS}
reply_tmpl: Dear {FIRST}, thank you for writing directly. I've asked our Village Engineer to evaluate the grade and inlet capacity at {STREET} and {CROSS} and report back within two weeks. You'll receive a follow-up from Tom Reyes, P.E. We take long-standing drainage issues seriously and I appreciate your patience.

subject_tmpl: Question about sump pump discharge rules — {ADDRESS}
body_tmpl: Hello, I'm {FIRST} {LAST} at {ADDRESS}. My sump pump runs constantly in spring and the discharge hose currently drains onto my driveway, which then flows to the street. A neighbor told me this might be against village code. Can you clarify the rules? I'd also like to know if there's a better place to direct the outflow — maybe toward the rear yard or a dry well. Thanks.
reply_tmpl: Hi {FIRST}, good question. Village code requires sump discharge to drain away from the foundation and not onto neighboring property. Discharging to the driveway and ultimately the street is generally acceptable as long as it doesn't create a nuisance. A dry well or rain garden is a great alternative — our office has design guides we can email you. Feel free to call {PHONE} if you'd like to chat.

subject_tmpl: Sewer smell coming from drain at {ADDRESS}
body_tmpl: I'm {FIRST} {LAST} and I live at {ADDRESS}. Starting around {DATE} I've been noticing a strong sewage odor coming up through my basement floor drain, especially in the evenings. There's no backup yet but the smell is really bad. Is this a sign of a blocked main? Could it be a dry trap? I'm not sure who to call first — thought Public Works would be the right place to start.

subject_tmpl: Ditch on {STREET} is overgrown and backing up
body_tmpl: The open ditch running along the east side of {STREET} between {CROSS} and the next block north is completely choked with vegetation and doesn't appear to have been maintained in years. After any significant rain it backs up and water spreads across the right-of-way and into my side yard at {ADDRESS}. I've got photos if they'd help. Who is responsible for maintaining that ditch — the village or the county?
reply_tmpl: Hello {FIRST}, the ditch you're describing falls within village right-of-way and Public Works is responsible for its maintenance. We'll schedule a crew for vegetation clearing and re-grading within the next {DAYS} days. If you can send your photos to pw@bellwoodil.gov, that would help our crew locate the worst sections.

subject_tmpl: Water standing in alley behind {ADDRESS} for days
body_tmpl: {NAME} at {ADDRESS}. The alley behind my house has had standing water in it since the rain on {DATE} — today is {DATE} and it's still there. The alley drain is clearly clogged. The water is turning green and the mosquitoes are awful. Can someone come clear that drain soon? I have {AGE}-year-old kids who play back there.

subject_tmpl: Repeated basement seepage — Village needs a long-term plan
body_tmpl: Mayor Okonkwo, I'm {FIRST} {LAST}, {ADDRESS}. My basement has flooded {NUM} times in the past two years. Each time I call Public Works they come, clear a drain, and say everything is fine — then it floods again. I've spent over ${AMOUNT} on cleanup and a new sump pump. This tells me there's a systemic problem in this neighborhood, not just a one-time clog. What is the village doing about aging sewer infrastructure on the west side? I deserve a real answer.
reply_tmpl: Dear {FIRST}, I hear your frustration and I'm sorry for the repeated disruption and expense. I've asked our Engineer to pull the sewer camera inspection records for your block and schedule a new inspection this month. If there is a structural defect in the main, we will include it in our capital planning. Someone from my office will contact you by {DATE}.

subject_tmpl: New development on {STREET} — worried about drainage impact
body_tmpl: Hello, I'm {FIRST} {LAST} on {STREET}. A new building is going up nearby and I'm concerned about how it will affect stormwater runoff into our block. Our yards already drain poorly. Did the village require a stormwater management plan for that project? If so, could I get a copy or at least an explanation of what protections are in place? I want to understand before the problem gets worse.

subject_tmpl: Storm drain grate broken at {STREET} & {CROSS} — safety concern
body_tmpl: Hi there. I noticed the storm drain grate at {STREET} and {CROSS} is cracked and partially sunken into the opening. It's a real tripping hazard and it also means the opening is partially exposed. Kids walk past there every morning for school. Can this be replaced soon? I reported it once on {DATE} but I never heard anything back. — {FIRST} {LAST}
reply_tmpl: Hi {FIRST}, thank you for following up. We've put this on our priority list for grate replacement this week. In the meantime, our crew will place a temporary marker at the location. We apologize for the delayed response to your earlier report.

subject_tmpl: Sewer camera inspection needed — {STREET} block
body_tmpl: Frank, I've had {NUM} resident complaints about recurring basement backups on the {STREET} block between {CROSS} and the next intersection. I'd like to get a camera run on the 8-inch main there before the next rain event. Can you schedule that in the next two weeks and send me the footage? I want to confirm whether the root intrusion we saw in {DATE} has gotten worse. Let me know what's on your crew's calendar.
reply_tmpl: Tom — I can get a crew out there the week of {DATE}. I'll have the tech send you the footage link same day. We're pretty backed up on service calls so I may need to push a routine inspection to fit this in. Will loop you in once it's on the schedule.

subject_tmpl: Stormwater capital project update — {STREET} detention basin
body_tmpl: Karen, quick update on the {STREET} detention basin project. Verdi Excavating has completed rough grading and the outlet control structure is scheduled for installation the week of {DATE}. We're still waiting on MWRD concurrence for the outlet pipe size — I've emailed our contact twice. If we don't hear by {DATE} I'll escalate. Overall we're about {NUM} days behind original schedule but I still think we can hit the fall substantial completion date.

subject_tmpl: Crew report: {STREET} inlet cleaning complete
body_tmpl: Tom, crew finished cleaning the {NUM} catch basins on {STREET} this morning. Removed significant debris from {NUM} of them — those were almost fully blocked. One inlet at {CROSS} has a cracked barrel that needs masonry repair; I've flagged it for the next round of capital maintenance. Let me know if you want before/after photos for the file. All basins are flowing freely now.

subject_tmpl: Standing water and mosquito breeding — alley block near {STREET}
body_tmpl: Frank, I've received {NUM} complaints about standing water in the alley west of {STREET} near {CROSS}. I inspected on {DATE} and found water that's been sitting at least {DAYS} days — a clear mosquito breeding risk. Health code requires we abate this. Can Public Works clear that alley drain ASAP? I'll follow up with larvicide treatment once the standing water is gone, but drainage fix has to come first.
reply_tmpl: Priya — I'll get a crew there by end of day {DATE}. If that drain needs more than a flush to clear, I'll call you so you can decide on interim larvicide. Thanks for the heads-up.

subject_tmpl: Water in basement — is this a sewer backup or my service line?
body_tmpl: Hello, I'm {FIRST} at {ADDRESS}, account {ACCT}. After Sunday's storm I had water come up through my basement floor drain. I can't tell if this is a sanitary sewer backup or something with my own service line. How do I find out? And if it's the village's sewer main, who covers the cleanup cost? I don't want to file an insurance claim if the village is responsible. Please advise.

subject_tmpl: Neighbor's sump is draining into my yard — {ADDRESS}
body_tmpl: {FIRST} {LAST}, {ADDRESS}. My neighbor's sump pump discharge hose runs right along the property line and dumps into a low spot in my yard. Every time the pump kicks on, water pools near my foundation. I've asked them to redirect it twice and nothing has changed. Is there a village ordinance about where sump water can be discharged? Can someone from the village help resolve this?
reply_tmpl: Hi {FIRST}, yes, village code prohibits sump discharge that causes nuisance conditions on neighboring property. Our code enforcement officer can issue a notice to the neighbor to redirect their discharge within {DAYS} days. We'll open a case — you should receive a case number by email shortly.

subject_tmpl: Flooding complaint — {STREET} neighborhood ignored for years
body_tmpl: Mayor Okonkwo — I am writing on behalf of several households on {STREET} who are fed up. We have reported drainage problems in this neighborhood every year for at least {AGE} years. The catch basins flood, the alley holds water for a week, and now two of us have had sewage back up into our homes. We are not going to accept more promises. We want a meeting with you and a commitment to fix this by {DATE}. Please respond.
reply_tmpl: I appreciate you writing and I understand your frustration completely. I am scheduling a neighborhood walk-through for {DATE} at {TIME} and would ask that you and your neighbors join me and our Public Works Director. We will review each problem location on-site and I will commit to a written action plan within 30 days of that meeting.

subject_tmpl: FEMA floodplain question — {ADDRESS}
body_tmpl: Hi, I'm {FIRST} {LAST}. I'm buying a home at {ADDRESS} and my mortgage lender is asking whether the property is in a FEMA-designated floodplain. Can the village confirm this? I looked on the FEMA map site but the panel numbers were confusing. Also, my real estate agent mentioned Bellwood has done some floodplain remapping — is that accurate? Thanks for any help.

subject_tmpl: Catch basin lid missing on {STREET} near {CROSS}
body_tmpl: I was walking on {STREET} near {CROSS} this morning and noticed a storm drain with no grate at all — just a big open hole in the curb line. A small child or animal could easily fall in. This is on the south side of {STREET} about half a block east of {CROSS}. Please send someone right away. I don't know when the grate went missing.
reply_tmpl: Thank you for alerting us immediately. We're dispatching a crew within the hour to secure the opening and assess the situation. A replacement grate will be installed by end of day. We appreciate you looking out for the community.

subject_tmpl: Flooding in parking lot — {BIZ}, {ADDRESS}
body_tmpl: This is {FIRST} {LAST}, owner of {BIZ} at {ADDRESS}. Every heavy rain event floods our parking lot to the point that customers can't park. The storm drain at the rear of the lot is on village property. I've had it cleared twice at my own expense but the problem keeps coming back. We lose business every time it rains. Can the village investigate whether the drain is undersized or whether the main it connects to is the issue? I'm happy to meet on-site.

subject_tmpl: Stormwater runoff from road work affecting {BIZ}
body_tmpl: Hello, I manage {BIZ} at {ADDRESS} on St. Charles Rd. Since the road resurfacing work began on {DATE}, runoff during rain events has been directed toward our entrance in a way it never was before. The contractor's equipment blocked the normal drainage path and now we're getting 3–4 inches of water at our front door. Can the village make Verdi Excavating address this before more rain comes?
reply_tmpl: Thank you for letting us know, {FIRST}. We've notified the contractor and our inspector will be on-site by {TIME} today to evaluate the temporary drainage condition. The contractor is required under their permit to maintain stormwater management throughout construction.

subject_tmpl: Ice forming over storm drain on {STREET} — slip hazard
body_tmpl: {FIRST} {LAST}, {ADDRESS}. The storm drain on {STREET} near {CROSS} has been partially blocked all fall, and now that temperatures have dropped, water is sheeting over the sidewalk and freezing solid. I nearly fell this morning and so did an elderly neighbor. The ice patch is about {NUM} feet across. Can you get the drain cleared and the ice treated today? This is a liability waiting to happen.

subject_tmpl: Rain garden program — is Bellwood participating?
body_tmpl: Hi, I'm {FIRST} {LAST} on {STREET}. I heard there are county or state programs that help homeowners install rain gardens to manage runoff. Does Bellwood participate in any cost-share or rebate program for that? My backyard holds water after every storm and I'm interested in a more permanent solution. Even a list of approved contractors or design standards would help. Thank you.
reply_tmpl: Hi {FIRST}, great question. Bellwood participates in the Cook County Rain Ready program, which offers free site assessments and some cost-sharing for qualifying installations. I can send you the program brochure and the contact info for the county coordinator. Would {DATE} work for a brief call to discuss your site?

subject_tmpl: IEPA stormwater grant — budget match needed by {DATE}
body_tmpl: David, the IEPA Section 319 grant we applied for requires a {NUM}% local match. The award amount is ${AMOUNT} which means our match is ${AMOUNT}. I need to confirm by {DATE} that the match is available in the stormwater fund. Can you let me know the current fund balance and whether the board has authority to commit those dollars or if we need a resolution? Grant conditions require a signed commitment letter from the Village Manager.
reply_tmpl: Tom, the stormwater fund currently has a balance of ${AMOUNT}. I can transfer the match amount to the project account once Karen signs off. I'll draft the commitment letter language for her review — please send me the grant award document so I can confirm the exact figure.

subject_tmpl: Water seeping through basement wall after {DATE} storm
body_tmpl: I'm {NAME}, homeowner at {ADDRESS}. After the storm on {DATE} I noticed water coming through the foundation wall on the north side of my basement, not the floor drain. I've never had this happen in {AGE} years of living here. There's a new retaining wall going up on the property behind me — could that be redirecting water toward my house? I don't know where to start. Should I call the village or a private contractor?

subject_tmpl: Blocked culvert on {STREET} causing yard flooding
body_tmpl: The culvert pipe under the driveway apron at the end of {STREET} near {CROSS} appears to be fully blocked with silt and debris. During the {DATE} rain the ditch water backed up and flooded three yards including mine at {ADDRESS}. I could see the water just sitting there with nowhere to go. Is this the village's responsibility to clear? I'm happy to send photos.
reply_tmpl: Hello {FIRST}, yes, culverts within the village right-of-way are our responsibility. We'll send a crew to jet and inspect that culvert within {DAYS} business days. Please do send photos to pw@bellwoodil.gov — they'll help the crew assess before they arrive.

subject_tmpl: Thank you — drainage work on {STREET} made a difference
body_tmpl: Mayor Okonkwo — I just wanted to say thank you. My name is {FIRST} {LAST} and I live at {ADDRESS}. The village crew came out and cleared the catch basins and re-graded the ditch on {STREET} last month. We had {NUM} inches of rain last week and for the first time in years, no standing water in my yard and no backup in my basement. Please pass along my thanks to whoever did the work. It really does make a difference.
reply_tmpl: Dear {FIRST}, thank you so much for taking the time to write. I'll make sure Frank DiMeo and the Public Works crew hear this — they work hard and this kind of feedback means a lot to them. Glad we could make a real difference.

subject_tmpl: Snowmelt draining poorly on {STREET} — possible blockage
body_tmpl: Hi, this is {FIRST} {LAST}, {ADDRESS}. With all the snow melting this week, I'm noticing the water isn't draining off {STREET} properly. It's pooling near the corner of {CROSS} and turning to slush that refreezes at night. I suspect the storm drains are still clogged from fall leaf season. Can a crew check them? I'd rather deal with it now before the next cold snap makes it worse.

subject_tmpl: Sewage smell at the corner of {STREET} and {CROSS}
body_tmpl: {FIRST} {LAST} here. There's been an intermittent sewage odor at the corner of {STREET} and {CROSS} for the past week. It's strongest in the morning before {TIME}. I can't tell if it's coming from a sewer vent or a partially open manhole. It's unpleasant enough that I've started walking a different route. Can someone check the manholes in that area?
reply_tmpl: Thank you {FIRST}. We'll send a crew to inspect manholes and sewer vents at that intersection within {DAYS} days. Odor complaints can indicate a seal or vent issue and we take them seriously. We'll update you once the inspection is complete.

subject_tmpl: Emergency sewer repair needed — {STREET} main collapse
body_tmpl: Karen — camera inspection on {STREET} this morning confirmed a partial collapse of the 8-inch vitrified clay main near the {CROSS} intersection. We have a crew on standby and I've reached out to Verdi Excavating for an emergency quote. Estimated cost is around ${AMOUNT} for open-cut repair. I need authorization to exceed the normal procurement threshold given the emergency nature — this main serves {NUM} properties. Can you approve this or does it need board authorization?
reply_tmpl: Frank, given the emergency nature of a confirmed collapse, I'm authorizing you to proceed up to ${AMOUNT} under emergency procurement provisions in the municipal code. Please document everything and we'll ratify it at the next board meeting. Keep me posted on timeline.

subject_tmpl: High water bill — wondering if it's related to flooding — Acct {ACCT}
body_tmpl: Hello, I'm {FIRST} {LAST} at {ADDRESS}, account {ACCT}. My water bill for the period ending {DATE} was ${AMOUNT}, which is about double my normal amount. Around the same time my basement had water in it after a storm. Could there be a connection? Is it possible the flooding somehow affected my meter or my service line? I want to make sure I'm only being billed for water I actually used.
reply_tmpl: Hi {FIRST}, a storm backup typically wouldn't affect your metered usage, but a service line leak could explain both higher usage and water intrusion. We can schedule a meter read and have a tech check your service line at no charge. In the meantime, check if any toilets are running continuously. Call our billing line at 708-547-3510 to set up the appointment.

subject_tmpl: IDOT coordination — Mannheim Rd drainage improvements
body_tmpl: Tom, following up on our call about the Mannheim Road resurfacing project. IDOT's drainage design proposes extending a cross-culvert that will increase flow into your storm system near {CROSS}. We need written confirmation from Bellwood that your downstream infrastructure can accept the additional capacity before we finalize the design. Can you provide a letter by {DATE} or let me know what information you need from us to make that determination?
reply_tmpl: Thanks for the follow-up. I'll review the hydraulic model you sent and get our Village Engineer's sign-off. We may need to confirm pipe sizes in the field before I can give you a firm letter. I'll have a response to you by {DATE} — please send the updated design drawings when they're ready.

subject_tmpl: Drainage swale being filled in by neighbor — {ADDRESS}
body_tmpl: This is {FIRST} {LAST} at {ADDRESS}. My neighbor has been filling in the drainage swale between our properties with soil and rocks, apparently to level their yard. The swale used to carry runoff from three lots to the street. Now that water has nowhere to go and my basement is wet for the first time. I believe they need a permit for grading changes. Can the village look into this?

subject_tmpl: Vacant lot on {STREET} draining onto my property — {CASE}
body_tmpl: Hello, I opened case {CASE} last month about the vacant lot at {ADDRESS} that drains toward my property at {ADDRESS}. An officer came out and said the lot owner would be notified. That was {DAYS} days ago and nothing has changed. The lot is still a bowl that fills with water and drains under the fence into my backyard. Can you give me an update on the case status?
reply_tmpl: Hi {FIRST}, I apologize for the lack of communication. I reviewed case {CASE} — a notice was mailed to the property owner on {DATE} with a {DAYS}-day compliance window. That window expires {DATE}. If the condition is not corrected by then, we will issue a citation and the village can proceed with abatement. I'll call you directly once I have an update.

subject_tmpl: Water main break or drainage problem? — {STREET}
body_tmpl: {NAME} here, calling about {STREET} near {CROSS}. There's water bubbling up from the pavement on the west curb lane. I can't tell if it's a water main leak or groundwater coming up. It started sometime this morning. The pavement around it is soft. I'm worried about the street sinking further. Please send someone as soon as possible.
reply_tmpl: Thank you for calling this in. We're dispatching a crew now to assess whether this is a main break or groundwater infiltration. We'll barricade the area until the cause is confirmed. Expect someone on-site within {NUM} minutes.

subject_tmpl: Request for backflow prevention device — {ADDRESS}
body_tmpl: I'm {FIRST} {LAST} at {ADDRESS}. After having my basement back up twice this year I've been told by a plumber that I should install a backflow prevention valve on my sanitary line. I've read that the village sometimes offers rebates or assistance for this. Is that program still active? And do I need a village permit to have it installed? I want to do this the right way.
reply_tmpl: Hi {FIRST}, yes, a permit is required for backflow preventer installation — it's a minor plumbing permit through the Building Department. We currently offer a rebate of up to ${AMOUNT} for qualifying installations. Call the Building Department at 708-547-3520 to apply. Make sure your plumber is licensed in Illinois.

subject_tmpl: Stormwater from construction site running down {STREET}
body_tmpl: Hi, {FIRST} {LAST} on {STREET}. A construction site at {ADDRESS} has no silt fence or other erosion controls. Every time a truck leaves the site it tracks mud into {STREET}, and when it rains the muddy runoff heads straight for the storm drain at {CROSS}. Isn't there a requirement for erosion control at construction sites? I've taken photos on {DATE} and {DATE}.

subject_tmpl: Grading permit review needed — {ADDRESS}
body_tmpl: Tom, I have a grading permit application for {ADDRESS} sitting on my desk. The owner wants to regrade the rear yard and install a new drainage pipe that ties into the village storm sewer in the alley. I need your signoff on the proposed connection point and pipe sizing before I can approve. Application submitted {DATE}. Can you review and return comments by {DATE}? Owner is eager to proceed before wet season.
reply_tmpl: Rosa, I'll take a look this week. If the connection is to the alley main it'll need to meet our standard detail for lateral connections. I'll either approve or request revised drawings — should have comments back to you by {DATE}.

subject_tmpl: Flooding near Metra station on {DATE} — commuters stranded
body_tmpl: Mayor Okonkwo — I'm {FIRST} {LAST} and I commute from the Bellwood Metra station. On {DATE} after the afternoon storm, the underpass at {CROSS} flooded and I had to wait {NUM} minutes for the water to recede to get to my car. Several commuters were standing on platforms with no way out. This seems like a known low point. Is there a plan to address the flooding at that underpass? It happens at least once a season.

subject_tmpl: Broken downspout extension flooding my walk — {ADDRESS}
body_tmpl: {FIRST} {LAST} at {ADDRESS}. This is a quick one: the city's sidewalk repair crew broke my downspout extension when they replaced the apron last fall. The water from my gutters now dumps right onto the sidewalk instead of out to the parkway. It's a slip hazard when it freezes. Can someone come look at it and make it right? I have photos from before and after the concrete work.
reply_tmpl: Hi {FIRST}, sorry to hear that happened during our sidewalk work. Please send your photos to pw@bellwoodil.gov and reference this complaint. We'll review with the crew and arrange for a replacement extension at no cost to you if the damage is confirmed to be from village work.

subject_tmpl: Ask about capital drainage project timeline — {STREET}
body_tmpl: Hello, I'm {FIRST} at {ADDRESS}. I attended the village board meeting on {DATE} where a drainage improvement project for the {STREET} area was approved. I live in that neighborhood and have dealt with chronic flooding for years. I'm writing to ask: when does construction start and how long will it take? Will residents be notified before any work in front of our homes? I want to plan around the disruption.
reply_tmpl: Hi {FIRST}, we're glad this project is finally moving. Design is being finalized now, with construction expected to begin around {DATE} and last approximately {NUM} months. Residents will receive a door-hanger notice at least {DAYS} days before work begins on their block. Feel free to reach out with questions as we get closer.

subject_tmpl: Heavy rain pooling at {STREET} & {CROSS} every storm
body_tmpl: I've lived near {STREET} and {CROSS} for {AGE} years and every significant rain produces the same result — water pools on all four corners to about {NUM} inches deep and stays there for hours. The drain grates look fine from outside. Maybe the pipe is undersized or partially blocked deeper down? I just moved back to Bellwood and want to see if anything has changed. Case from {DATE} if you still have records.

subject_tmpl: Sewer backup during storm — damage claim question — {ADDRESS}
body_tmpl: Hello, I'm {FIRST} {LAST}, {ADDRESS}. On {DATE} during the heavy rain, sewage backed up into my basement through the laundry drain. I had {NUM} inches of sewage-contaminated water and lost a washer, dryer, and a lot of stored belongings. Total damage is around ${AMOUNT}. I've been told I can file a claim against the village. Where do I send a formal claim and what documentation do I need? I have photos and contractor estimates.
reply_tmpl: We're sorry about your loss, {FIRST}. To file a claim against the village, please submit a written claim to the Village Clerk's office at Village Hall, 3200 Washington Blvd. Include photos, contractor estimates, and a list of damaged property with values. Claims are reviewed by our insurance carrier. You can also call the Village Clerk at 708-547-3500 ext. 201 for the claim form.

subject_tmpl: Pre-storm checklist reminder — forecast for {DATE}
body_tmpl: Frank, NWS is forecasting {NUM} to {NUM} inches of rain between {TIME} and {TIME} on {DATE}. Per our pre-storm protocol, please confirm that crews have cleared priority inlets on the {STREET} and {CROSS} corridors, the lift station at {CROSS} is operational, and on-call staffing is in place. Let me know if you have any capacity concerns by noon so I can alert the Village Manager if needed.
reply_tmpl: Tom — confirmed. Inlet crew went out this morning, lift station checked yesterday and all clear, on-call crew briefed for tonight. We've got pumps staged at {STREET} in case the low point floods. I'll text you if anything changes during the event.

subject_tmpl: Water pouring from adjacent vacant lot — {ADDRESS}
body_tmpl: {FIRST} {LAST}, {ADDRESS}. The vacant lot next door at {ADDRESS} appears to have had grading work done recently, and now stormwater runs directly off it and into my driveway and garage. Before, it drained away from my property. I don't know if someone had work done illegally or if this was a natural change. Either way, my garage flooded on {DATE} and I can't keep up with it. What are my options?
